blocks.content: The Limits of Discipline (3)

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Brother! Brother!
Wizard called out as she left the training ground, but Shirone seemed not to hear her and quickened his pace.
He arrived at the most secluded spot in the school, a place not designed for rest.
Having lived in the mountains since childhood, Shirone always sought nature when he wanted to relax.
Sitting on a small rock, he exhaled but couldn't release the frustration in his heart.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haa.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is the right thing to do?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: To kill Satan Havitz, Wizard's power was essential, but what about Wizard's life?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was a situation where a seven-year-old child had to kill the most evil and cruel person in the world.
Shirone chuckled.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maybe I'm the one who's crazy.
He had known this for some time, but this time it was different.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Brother.
At Wizard's voice, Shirone looked up.
She must have followed him out of concern, but seeing her with a splint added to his guilt.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You got hurt badly. Wizard, I need to tell you…

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I'm sorry, brother.
Her eyes welled up with tears.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I didn't think about how much Mom and Dad would hate it. Learning is so much fun that I got too excited…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fun?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, right.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter how hard and painful it was, humans could endure it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As long as they have the certainty that they can become stronger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wizard, why do you find magic fun? Do you want to be the strongest in the world? Do you want to surpass me?

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I want to surpass you. But now it doesn't matter if I win. Magic is just fun.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There are many ways to be happy in life. You can enjoy life without being the best. So why do you want to learn magic?

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Well, that's because…
And as always, she smiled the brightest smile in the world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, you're right.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter what others said, this was the only world we would live in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Only we could understand ourselves.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Today's training was a bit weak. I told Mom and Dad. It'll be fine from tomorrow.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Haha!
Shirone wiped away his tears.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Since diving into the world of magic, he had never experienced defeat…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, let's grit our teeth and run.
All the suppressed frustrations were being comforted for that one result.
Wizard approached Shirone and wiped away his tears with her small hand.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: You must be having a hard time too.
It hadn't been long since they intensified their training, but Wizard's insight had deepened considerably.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: You've grown a lot. Being able to understand others' feelings is an excellent talent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hehe. So, brother, are you in love with Amy?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Well…

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Then if I become stronger than you someday, will you like me more?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She's still a child.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There are many kinds of love. If you really surpass me…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If she became the world's greatest magician…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, there might be times when I think you're more precious than Amy.
It was an adult's way of categorizing things, but Wizard was delighted by the possibility.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Great! Then I'll surpass you tomorrow!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh really?
Shirone pulled Wizard's broken arm and healed it with Miracle Stream.
The next day, the atmosphere at the training ground was tense.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hoo, today will be intense too.
As teachers were already tense, Shirone and Wizard warmed up in front of the Image Zone.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Is it combat practice?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. Today is basics.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wizard's determination gave Shirone enough courage, but he couldn't ignore her parents' perspective.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Seeing their child in pain is the worst for parents. I was too thoughtless.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if it's our world, we must consider the feelings of those watching from outside.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Today's lesson is mental pulsation.
It was optimal training that wasn't overly intense while maintaining its rigor.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll demonstrate.
As an innate awakener, Wizard instinctively cast spells but lacked theoretical knowledge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's even more impressive, but to face Havitz, I must teach her everything I can.
When Wizard's creativity clashed with Havitz's chaos, her body would remember this moment.
Shirone stood at the center of the Image Zone installed at Stang National Magic School.
Memories of training to improve his grades in his final year flashed through his mind like a lantern show.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I must do well.
As his heartbeat unfolded without warning, teachers watching widened their eyes in amazement.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: What… what is this…
In the Image Zone, mental activity became visible, but there was no sign of back-and-forth movement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A giant sphere…? No, it's definitely oscillating. The count keeps rising.
Each time it reached its maximum radius, a buzzer blared through the speakers as if tearing them apart.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Heart rate: 6,000 beats per second.
Whoever was ranked second in the world as a magician couldn't match this speed by several times over.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: We can't compete with that.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: For someone who had mastered mental techniques, it was an expected result. But Wizard was different.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: She can't reach it. Even if she does, she'll usually die. The Five Great Stars are serious.
As Bartok turned his head, he saw Wizard watching intently without blinking at all during Shirone's demonstration.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: Aha.
The Ten Mages simultaneously realized something.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: 1 minute 17 seconds.
It took Wizard 1 minute 17 seconds to grasp what she saw from Shirone's 6,000 beats per second heart rate demonstration without knowing any basics beforehand.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: Got it?
Without needing further explanation, Wizard nodded and entered the image zone.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I'll give it a try.
Wizard positioned herself next to Amy, and behind them stood the Ten Mages of the Ivory Tower.
Though it was officially part of a training exercise, there was a genuine curiosity about how far Wizard could push herself.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Hoo…
After taking a deep breath, Wizard suddenly widened her eyes and deployed her Spirit Zone.
As the buzzer sounded, the teachers and students fixed their attention on her rapidly increasing heartbeat.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: 800 beats per second. This is far beyond a professional mage's level.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: However, we're looking for someone who can reach the pinnacle of the universe. At this level, it's still not enough…

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Whoa! What's going on?
As the students gasped in shock, Wizard's heartbeat rate continued climbing.
2,000 beats per second.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: The pupils of the Ten Mages of the Ivory Tower trembled, but the situation was still within acceptable limits.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: She's maintaining control.
The heartbeat continued to rise, and blood trickled down from Wizard's nose.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: Dear…
Wizard's mother grabbed her husband's arm nervously.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Faster… I can go faster.
Perhaps unaware of the extreme changes happening within her own body, Wizard kept pushing herself further.
At 3,000 beats per second, blood began seeping from her eyes. Her mother, unable to bear it any longer, rushed toward Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: Stop! She's going to die if this continues!

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: I am… a one-star resident of the Ivory Tower.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: Although skilled enough to be a magic association president in any kingdom, he still fell short of the two-star, three-star, four-star, and five-star levels above him.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: Who wouldn't desire to climb higher? Who wouldn't crave it? But simply wishing for it doesn't make it possible to reach.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: That's enough. As we told you yesterday, our daughter's safety is our top priority.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: They don't understand.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: They couldn't comprehend the desperation of those obsessed with becoming stronger in this world.
Mental pulse: 4,000 beats per second.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: It's not about defeating someone else. It's not even about gaining something by becoming stronger. It's the pure joy of rising endlessly, the happiness of knowing that you can continue to grow.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: In other words: All humans are…

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Aaaahhh!
Mental pulse: 5,000 beats per second.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: I can get stronger. I can do better. Believe. The moment you start doubting, everything crumbles.
Her tears mixed with the blood streaming from her eyes—tears of pure longing.

blocks.speaker: Bartok
blocks.content: I… want to soar through the sky.
As Wizard reached her physical and mental limits, her Spirit Zone began to distort and collapse.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If you lose your rhythm, it's over.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Your thoughts are too rigid. Let go of everything—your desire to be recognized, your obsession with success—release it all and immerse yourself in the present. Don't consolidate your thoughts; let them flow outward. Pour yourself out.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Arghhh!
When her mental pulse reached 6,000 beats per second, Shirone felt a thrill of awe.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She immediately understands and shifts her mental state.
There was no room for jealousy—what Shirone felt was akin to witnessing a masterpiece, pure and inspiring.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: Please, wizard…

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: Please stop this. We understand that our daughter is talented, but… what's the point of success if it means enduring so much pain?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Talent?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Do you think she's enduring all this pain because she has talent? If that's what you believe, then you're gravely underestimating Wizard.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: But… but…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, Wizard is a genius. Maybe talent does exist. But, ma'am…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's no such thing in this world as talent that allows someone to excel without effort.
The mother fell silent at the conviction in his voice.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: All humans are the same. Do you think geniuses feel less pain when pricked by a needle? No. The difference lies in how they perceive and process that pain. Are you watching Wizard's talent? I see something different. I see a seven-year-old child who endured immense suffering just to reach this point.
Shirone turned his gaze back to Wizard.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She's here because of the sheer amount of pain she's been processing since she was in her mother's womb.
How much had she cried as a fetus, trying to process such incomprehensible sensations?

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Mother
blocks.content: Our daughter…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You said you're farmers.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know much about farming, but I heard farmers remove rotten seedlings in advance. It must not feel good. Nurturing life on a grand scale can sometimes be cruel.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: We can't expect you to understand our world, just as we can't fully understand yours. But within that world, we dream and compete. For us, that's the only true world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wizard wants to be the best. Not because she has talent, but because the immense suffering she's endured won't allow her to accept her current state. That's why, the moment she met me, she gave it her all.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please, entrust her to me. If I judge that she truly cannot endure any longer, I will stop.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: But she's already bleeding so much…

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: Be quiet.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: I understand. I'm a man, too. I've dreamt of standing at the top of the world. But you're telling me… that our daughter can be the best?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. If she can continue processing this kind of pain endlessly, she will be.
The father looked at Wizard, who was gritting her teeth, blood streaming from her eyes, yet still holding on.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: Damn it! Of course, she wants to fly. I wanted to fly too. So go ahead—give it your all. I'll protect you.

blocks.speaker: Wizard's Father
blocks.content: Please, take good care of her!
Closing his eyes tightly, he bowed his head and shouted.
